title: External Anchor Text Guidelines
content_md:
# External Anchor Text Guidelines

- Code: c73971e8
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=c73971e882f449a4b36db3917e99a29f
- Video: https://www.loom.com/share/32e064265e9144b88df3a4c1e13f5d3e?sid=d3917478-5a11-4359-bb4f-9a5a3526cd76?hide_owner=true&hide_share=true&hide_title=true&hideEmbedTopBar=true
- Duration: 2m 59s

## Description

_(no description)_

transcript.text: So external anchor text the overall message here is going to be pretty similar to what we said for internal anchor text
We want it to look natural. We don't want it to look like a
Professional SEO has sourced all of this anchor text. So general guidelines. We really do not want to repeat anchor text
Okay, very long anchor text that is repeated over and over again is a sign of spam
Just try to avoid repeating anchor text the exception to that is exact match and in that only if you have a lot of links coming in
So you might repeat exact match once or twice
But again, that's only if you have like 20 30 40 links coming into a site then you might repeat the exact match
Keywords if you when you're using keywords you want to incorporate them naturally
They have to fit in with the flow of the content on the page that has the link
We know that Google
Reads the content around the link to decide what the link is about and not just the anchor text itself
So make sure that your link is in the section of content that is talking about the destination page if possible
Similarly match the destination URL don't have the anchor text talk about something that isn't related to the URL
It's linking to you will want to use some generic anchor text
Click here read more that is much more common for external anchor text than for internal
I remember for internal we said not to use generic anchor text. That's not true for external. We want to use generic
We want the anchor text in the body
We don't want to put it in the footer or the sidebar or heading tag
We just in the body of the text is the best place for them
And also we want to use some branding and some just naked URLs
I know we said not to do that for internal but for external. We definitely want to so the guidance for how much of each to use
30% of the link should be branded and 20% or so should be generic click here or similar
So that's about half of the external anchor text would not be keyword rich and not include any keywords on it at all
a 30% of it
Keyword rich partial match. So there you're going to add like extra words or something like that
so
So that you have keyword rich
But it is not the exact match keyword you're trying to rank and here you have 5% exact match again
You might have two or three that repeat that exact match anchor text
If you have a bunch of links coming in and then the 15% for natural variations. So this wouldn't include the keyword
But it would include a natural variation of that keyword like lawyer attorney type of
Variation so keep these rules in mind as you do your external anchor text these percentages are guidelines
Really the focus is to make sure that it is a natural looking link profile

summary: This lesson explains how to create natural and effective external anchor text for SEO purposes. It emphasizes avoiding repeated keywords, using a mix of branded, generic, and keyword-rich links, and ensuring the content flows naturally with the page’s context.
key_points: Use 30% branded, 20% generic, 5% exact match, 55% natural variations
Ensure anchor text fits naturally within the content
